findings.title: N+1 Query Pattern in /api/kpi (Dashboard Summary)
findings.trace: PUT /api/kpi fetches creators table with .select('*') then iterates in JavaScript to filter by tier/source: tierBreakdown, sourceBreakdown calculated via .filter() on full result set
findings.scenario: Current: ~30K creators = 5 database queries + O(30K) JS iteration = ~150-200ms. Projected at 30K: ~200-250ms, plus memory cost of 30K creator objects (~150KB). At 100K creators: ~800ms+ timeout risk.
findings.impact: Dashboard load blocked on KPI summary. Each page load triggers 5 parallel queries, 4 of which are unfiltered full table scans. Peak load causes connection pool exhaustion.

fix.description: Use SQL GROUP BY and aggregate functions instead of JS iteration. Reduces query count from 5 to 1 and eliminates N+1 filtering.

findings.title: Unbounded Query in /api/creator (Personal Dashboard)
findings.trace: GET /api/creator?id=X fetches all content_tracking records with no LIMIT: .from('content_tracking').select('*').eq('creator_id', creatorId). Then computes avgCtr/avgCvr on entire result set.
findings.scenario: Creator with 100 videos = 100 rows + network transfer + JS averaging = ~50ms. Creator with 500 videos (highly active) = ~200ms. At scale with 30K creators, top 1K creators (~500 vids each) = 200ms response per creator load.
findings.impact: Slow personal dashboard. Creator loads all their content on every dashboard view. Memory spikes for high-volume creators (500 videos = ~200KB per request).

fix.description: Add LIMIT(30) for recent videos, use computed monthly metrics from creators table instead of aggregating on each request.

findings.title: Sequential Sync Jobs Block Each Other in runFullSync()
findings.trace: runFullSync() executes 4 jobs sequentially: syncProfiles() → syncContent() → syncShopOrders() → computeMetrics(). Each fetches creators, sleeps on external API calls, then updates DB.
findings.scenario: Current at 2K creators: syncProfiles(50 batch) = 100ms × 40 batches = 4s, syncContent(30 batch) = 150ms × 66 batches = 10s, syncShopOrders() = 5-10s (API pagination), computeMetrics() = 3s. Total: ~25-30 seconds. At 30K creators: 5-7 minutes. At 100K: 15-20+ minutes, likely timeout.
findings.impact: Daily 3AM sync job can run 20+ minutes, overlapping with subsequent 6AM and 9AM jobs. Blocks dashboard updates during peak creator hours.

fix.description: Parallelize profile and content sync (independent operations), use database cursors for pagination instead of client-side batching.

findings.title: Missing Database Indexes for Common Filters
findings.trace: Indexes exist for tier, status, source, but missing for common query patterns: monthly_gmv DESC (used in /api/creators sort), created_at (used in all list endpoints), content_tracking.posted_at with creator_id.
findings.scenario: Query: creators.select('*').order('monthly_gmv DESC').limit(20) on 30K rows without index = full table scan + sort = ~500-800ms. With index: ~10-20ms. At 100K creators without index: 2-3 seconds.
findings.impact: Leaderboards, creator list views, all sorting operations are slow. Dashboard becomes unresponsive during traffic spikes.

fix.description: Add composite and covering indexes for frequently sorted/filtered columns.

findings.title: Reminder Engine Iterative Updates (O(n) Database Calls)
findings.trace: processReminders() fetches delivered shipments, then for EACH shipment loops: sendReminder1, sendReminder2, markNoResponse. Each iteration calls supabase.update() individually. At 1000 delivered shipments: 1000+ update calls to database.
findings.scenario: 1000 delivered shipments = 1000 individual UPDATE queries = ~1-2ms each = 1-2 seconds. At 10K shipments: 10-20 seconds. Each reminder also triggers sendDM/sendEmail which may be async but blocks DB write.
findings.impact: Reminder cron job (runs daily) becomes slow, may timeout if 10K+ shipments pending. Blocks database connection pool during peak times.

fix.description: Use SQL CASE/WHEN to batch-update all shipments in 1-3 queries instead of iterating. Pre-calculate reminder deadlines in SQL.

findings.title: Recommendation Engine Fetches Top 10 Creators Per User (N+1 Pattern)
findings.trace: For each creator calling generateRecommendations(), computeComparison() fetches top 10 creators in same tier. When called for 30K creators, triggers 30K queries to fetch top creators, but result is same for all creators in tier.
findings.scenario: 10 creators in 'gold' tier = 10 identical queries fetching top 10 gold creators = 10 DB queries that return same 10 rows. At 30K creators (5K per tier): 5K × 10 bronze + 3K × 10 silver queries = 80K+ queries total. At 100K: 250K+ queries.
findings.impact: Recommendation generation for bulk creator operations becomes prohibitively slow. Cron job to refresh all recommendations would take hours.

fix.description: Cache tier-level statistics (median views, top format) in separate metrics table, compute once per day instead of per creator.

findings.title: Missing LIMIT in /api/creators and /api/outreach List Endpoints
findings.trace: Both routes accept search/filter parameters but fetch .select('*', { count: 'exact' }) without LIMIT before range(). At 100K creators, 'exact' count scans entire table.
findings.scenario: GET /api/creators?search=beauty&page=1 with 100K rows: exact count = full table scan (500-800ms) + 20 rows fetch = 600-900ms just for count. With 100 concurrent requests = 60-90 seconds of DB time per second of wall time.
findings.impact: List pages become slow at scale. count: 'exact' is blocking operation; pagination becomes unusable above 10K records.

fix.description: Remove count: 'exact' and use estimated count from EXPLAIN, or cache total count and update periodically.

findings.title: Sync Job Crawler Fetches 10 Videos Per Creator Without Deduplication
findings.trace: syncContent() fetches .limit(batchSize=30) creators, then for EACH calls crawler.fetchVideos({count: 10}). Result: 30 × 10 = 300 videos. If creator already has 200 videos in DB, fetches all 10 again and upserts, wasting bandwidth.
findings.scenario: 30K creators synced daily, 30 batch = 1000 rounds, each fetching 10 videos = 10K video API calls daily. If crawler API charges per call: 10K calls × $0.01/call = $100/day. With dedup: ~2K new videos only = $20/day.
findings.impact: High API costs, slow crawler. Network bandwidth wasted on refetching stale data.

fix.description: Track last_content_sync_at per creator, fetch only videos newer than last sync, add offset-based pagination.

findings.title: Dashboard Summary (KPI) Doesn't Use Row-Level Security Efficiently
findings.trace: Fetches samples.data and filters client-side for pendingSamples: .filter(s => ['requested', 'approved'].includes(s.status)). Fetches entire sample_shipments table, then filters in JS.
findings.scenario: 100K sample shipments, 10% pending = 10K fetched, 9K discarded = 2-3MB network transfer for unused rows. At 100 concurrent dashboard loads: 200-300MB/sec bandwidth wasted.
findings.impact: Network overhead, memory spikes, slower dashboard loads for all users.

fix.description: Filter at database level using .in('status', ['requested', 'approved']) instead of post-fetch filtering.

findings.title: Referral Engine Inefficient Code Uniqueness Check (Loop Until Success)
findings.trace: generateCode() loops up to 100 times to find unique code: while(counter < 100) { query DB, if exists counter++, retry }. At high volume (1000 new referrals/day), some codes require 10-20 tries.
findings.scenario: 1000 new codes, average 5 retries each = 5000 DB queries instead of 1. Each retry = ~2ms = 10 seconds wasted on retry logic that could be solved with SQL UNIQUE constraint.
findings.impact: Code generation endpoint slowdown during onboarding waves. Inefficient retry logic.

fix.description: Use SQL INSERT with ON CONFLICT UNIQUE to atomically handle uniqueness in database, not application.

findings.title: Inefficient autoDetectContentPosted() N+1 Query Pattern
findings.trace: autoDetectContentPosted() fetches all delivered shipments, then loops through EACH and queries content_tracking individually. For 1000 shipments: 1 initial fetch + 1000 individual content checks = 1001 DB queries.
findings.scenario: 1000 shipments = 1001 queries × ~2ms = 2 seconds. At 10K shipments: 20 seconds. Could be done in 1 query with LEFT JOIN.
findings.impact: Reminder engine blocks on slow content detection phase. Reminders may not send on time during peak hours.

fix.description: Use LEFT JOIN content_tracking in single query instead of loop-based lookups.
fix.diff:
--- a/src/lib/reminders/reminder-engine.ts
+++ b/src/lib/reminders/reminder-engine.ts
@@ -271,42 +271,36 @@ export class ReminderEngine {
   async autoDetectContentPosted(): Promise<number> {
     let detected = 0;
 
     try {
-      // Get all delivered shipments without content posted
       const { data: shipments, error } = await this.supabase
         .from('sample_shipments')
-        .select('*, creator:creators(id, tiktok_handle, tiktok_id)')
-        .eq('status', 'delivered')
-        .is('content_posted_at', null);
+        .select(`
+          id, delivered_at, creator_id,
+          content:content_tracking(
+            id, created_at, video_url
+          )
+        `)
+        .eq('status', 'delivered')
+        .is('content_posted_at', null)

findings.title: computeMetrics() Limits to 100 Creators per Call (Incomplete Coverage)
findings.trace: computeMetrics(creatorIds) hardcodes .limit(100), so with 30K active creators, requires 300 separate API calls to compute all metrics.
findings.scenario: 30K creators ÷ 100 limit = 300 rounds. Called once per sync cycle. If sync runs 1x/day: 300 requests × 500ms = 150 seconds of compute time.
findings.impact: Metrics computation takes too long, may not complete before next sync cycle. Metrics always stale.

fix.description: Remove arbitrary limit or increase to 500-1000, use server-side pagination with cursor-based approach.

summary: The banilaco-crew platform faces 12 critical-to-medium performance bottlenecks that will severely impact user experience at 30K creators. Primary issues: (1) N+1 query patterns in dashboard KPI aggregation and recommendation engine that scale quadratically, (2) unbounded full-table scans in list endpoints without pagination or indexed sorts, (3) sequential sync jobs that block each other, blocking 20+ minutes at 30K scale. Database indexes for monthly_gmv and created_at sorting are missing. Recommendation: implement SQL-level aggregations, batch database operations, parallelize independent syncs, add missing indexes, and remove count: 'exact' from paginated endpoints to achieve <500ms dashboard response times at 100K creators.
